programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc: Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will address the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations.

programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc:
Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will tackle the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations.
For years, the Iban people living in Borneo were considered one of the most terrifying communities in the world.Its members carried out ritual hunts for people.They believed that a warrior who shed blood gained strength and glory.Nowadays, this tradition is no longer cultivated, but its traces still remain in Iban homes.They never got rid of the trophies they won years ago.Martyna Wojciechowska talks to a descendant of bounty hunters.He also takes part in the skull taming ritual.

programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc:
Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will tackle the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations.
Martyna Wojciechowska talks to Morena Herrera, who fights for women's rights in El Salvador.The country ranks first in terms of the number of murders of women.About 500 such cases are reported annually, with a population of less than 3 million.More than half of the victims of violence do not seek help and do not reveal the truth even to their closest family members.Moreover, El Salvador has one of the most restrictive anti-abortion laws in the world.Termination of pregnancy is always illegal.If a woman loses a child in unclear circumstances, she immediately becomes a suspect.The penalty for her ranges from 2 to 8 years, and if found guilty, she may be imprisoned for 50 years.A doctor can be sentenced to 6 to 12 years for performing an abortion.Anyone who knew about the situation but did not inform the authorities is also complicit.

programs.title: By truck across the United States
programs.desc: Dawid Andres will once again take advantage of the opportunity to combine delivering goods with exploring American nature, architecture and culture.With him, viewers will visit, among others:Atlanta and check out the Daytona track.
